{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Shimanoyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawanishi, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the burial mounds that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the burial mounds that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Shimanoyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawanishi, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the burial mounds that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Shimanoyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawanishi, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>

It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Shimanoyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawanishi, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Shimanoyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawanishi, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shimanoyama Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01802.html Nara Prefecture History and Cultural Database ]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2018%20shimanoyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.nara-kawanishi.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000001/1803/04.pdf Kawanishi Town home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>

It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[Yamato River]] in the center of the [[Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in the center of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

Some 2500 items of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Shimanoyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in the center of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17213138]] Wikidata name: Shimanoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.583889|N|135.765722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in the center of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimanoyama Kofun]][[en:Shimanoyama Kofun]][[ja:島の山古墳]][[zh:島之山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17213138]] Wikidata name: Shimanoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.583889|N|135.765722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in the center of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimanoyama Kofun]][[en:Shimanoyama Kofun]][[ja:島の山古墳]][[zh:島之山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213138]] Wikidata name: Shimanoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimanoyama Kofun | native_name =島の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.583889|N|135.765722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|2|N|135|45|46.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimanoyama Kofun'''|島の山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tōin neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawanishi, Nara|Kawanishi]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3337|title=島の山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The items [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tumulus have been collectively designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]]. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198726|title=奈良県島の山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimanoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slight elevation overlooking the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in the center of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]] and is one of the Kofuns that make up the northern group of the Umami Kofun cluster. It is about one kilometer east of the [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster]]. The location of these tumuli is where the rivers of the Yamato Basin converge, and it is located at a key transportation point. As the shape and dimensions of the Kawai Otsukayama Tomb and the Shimanoyama Tomb are identical, these two tombs are thought to be closely related. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Shimanoyama Kofun is a large tumulus with a current length of 190 meters, a diameter of over 105 meters at the rear, and a width of over 95 meters at the front, and is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat about 40 meters wide. There are projections on the east and west narrow parts. As the tumulus has been eroded by water from the moat, it is estimated that it was originally over 200 meters in length. Archaeological excavations were carried out in 1996, and the main part of a clay coffin was unearthed in the anterior rectangular portion. This clay coffin was constructed in a grave pit 3.4 by 10.5 meters, and is about 2 by 8.5 meters in size. It contained 7.5 meter long split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Japanese umbrella pine]]. Inside the coffin, vermilion had been sprinkled over the area where the deceased had been placed. Some 2500 items of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, three stone boxes, and five tubular beads that were excavated near the head, tubular beads thought to be a necklace near the chest, and a bracelet with tubular and round beads attached near the wrist, as well as an iron knife and a vertical comb. The presence of the hand beads suggests that the buried person was a woman. An excavation of the circular mound area was carried out from 1997 to 1999, and the remains of the terraced construction, ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, as well as rows of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures (morning glory-shaped cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped) on the first and second terraces were confirmed. In addition, during the 10th survey conducted in 2005, a vegetable basket thought to have been used for rituals was excavated from the western narrow part. There appears to have been a vertical pit entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular mound, but it was destroyed by grave robbery. The monolithic ceiling stones of the burial chamber were brought to this location from a quarry in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the main body, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], and ''haniwa'' clay figures, this tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century, and is thought to be the tomb of a powerful chief in the Nara Basin due to its large size and abundant grave goods. It is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yūzaki Station|Yūzaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Shimanoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Shimanoyama Kofun 島の山古墳出土 車輪石.JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳 車輪石 (天理大学附属天理参考館所蔵).JPG|Wheel stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 鍬形石.JPG|Huwagata stone excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus 島の山古墳出土 石製合子.JPG|Stone bowl excavated from Shimanoyama tumulus </gallery>
